sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Saint-Étienne have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, while Toulouse have been slightly more consistent with 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses. Saint-Étienne's recent games have often seen high-scoring encounters, with 4 of their last 5 matches having over 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Toulouse won 2-1 at home, while the earlier match at Saint-Étienne ended in a 2-2 draw. Both games saw goals from both sides, indicating a trend of competitive and open play between these teams.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Saint-Étienne, positioned 17th, struggle defensively with 74 goals conceded, the highest in the league. Toulouse, at 12th, have a more balanced record with 41 goals scored and conceded, suggesting a slight edge in overall stability.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: Saint-Étienne miss key forward Lucas Stassin (12 goals), impacting their attack, though Irvin Cardona is in form with 3 goals in the last 5 matches. Toulouse have no major absences and rely on Zakaria Aboukhlal (7 goals) for attacking threat.

sections.subtitle: Match Dynamics
sections.text: Saint-Étienne's home matches often see high corner counts (average 3.88 per game), and their games frequently feature over 2.5 goals. Toulouse's away form suggests a cautious approach with fewer corners (trend of under 10.5) and a focus on counter-attacks.
